# UC-22.41.2 · Certificate / TLS posture — weak cipher and expired-cert detection

## Description

Continuous TLS-posture audit replacing periodic scan spreadsheets. Any finding feeds remediation and the cipher-and-cert evidence pack.

## Value

Moves TLS posture from 'we scan quarterly' to 'we proved every TLS endpoint was compliant for every day of the period'.

## Implementation

(1) Run scanner (SSL Labs CLI, OpenSSL-based scripts, Qualys SSL Labs); (2) feed to Splunk; (3) schedule hourly; (4) on finding, ticket via SOAR. Indexes required: `index=tls`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=tls:scan`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Run scanner (SSL Labs CLI, OpenSSL-based scripts, Qualys SSL Labs); (2) feed to Splunk; (3) schedule hourly; (4) on finding, ticket via SOAR.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=tls sourcetype=tls:scan earliest=-1h
| eval days_to_expiry=round((expires-now())/86400,0)
| where days_to_expiry<21 OR protocol!="TLSv1.2" AND protocol!="TLSv1.3" OR NOT match(cipher, "^TLS_(AES|CHACHA20|ECDHE)")
| table host days_to_expiry protocol cipher
```

Understanding this SPL

**Certificate / TLS posture — weak cipher and expired-cert detection** — Moves TLS posture from 'we scan quarterly' to 'we proved every TLS endpoint was compliant for every day of the period'.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: tls; **sourcetype**: tls:scan. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=tls, sourcetype=tls:scan, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_to_expiry** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_to_expiry<21 OR protocol!="TLSv1.2" AND protocol!="TLSv1.3" OR NOT match(cipher, "^TLS_(AES|CHACHA20|ECDHE)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: cipher, days_to_expiry, host, protocol.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of non-compliant hosts, stacked bar of protocol distribution, single-value '% of endpoints with strong TLS'.

## SPL

```spl
index=tls sourcetype=tls:scan earliest=-1h
| eval days_to_expiry=round((expires-now())/86400,0)
| where days_to_expiry<21 OR protocol!="TLSv1.2" AND protocol!="TLSv1.3" OR NOT match(cipher, "^TLS_(AES|CHACHA20|ECDHE)")
| table host days_to_expiry protocol cipher
```

## Visualization

Table of non-compliant hosts, stacked bar of protocol distribution, single-value '% of endpoints with strong TLS'.
